retrieval_text: Lobelia is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 235 works and 1669 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Lobelia, Lobelia Herb. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Seed, Leaf, Bark, Gum, Flower. Common preparation co-mentions include Tincture, Tea, Powder, Emetic, Oil, Syrup. Common condition co-mentions include Vomiting or nausea, Cold or catarrh, Cough, Wounds or ulcers, Fever, Inflammation. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Toxicity watch, Fatal-risk warning, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions; An improved system of domestic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on anatomy and physiology, the practice of medicine, with a copious materia medica , and an extensive treatise on midwifery, embellished with over one hundred useful engravings, gotten up expressly for family use; An improved system of domestic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on anatomy and physiology, the practice of medicine, with a copious materia medica, and an extensive treatise on midwifery, embellished with over one hundred useful engravings, gotten up expressly for family use / by Horton Howard.; An improved system of botanic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on the practice of medicine with a copious materia medica. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
